name: Satay Kuah Sauce (Spicy Peanut Sauce)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the outer layer from lemon grass and remove tough tops (save for soup stock if desired). Chop tender stalks into 1-inch lengths.
2. Place chopped lemon grass, shallots, garlic, coriander, hot chili sauce, cumin seeds, and turmeric in a food processor. Whirl to a fine paste, adding water as needed.
3. Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the lemon grass paste and dried shrimp paste, stirring and frying for about 4 minutes or until the mixture emits an aromatic perfume.
4. Stir in about half of the coconut milk and continue cooking, stirring almost constantly, for 8 minutes.
5. Stir in the remaining coconut milk, sweeten with brown sugar, and season with salt.
6. Blend in roasted peanuts. Taste to adjust sweetness, salt, and hotness as desired. Thin with more coconut milk or water if preferred.
